quick_facts.label: Programme format
quick_facts.value: B.Sc. LL.B. (Hons.) is a five-year integrated undergraduate law degree. Examples include NLIU Bhopal’s B.Sc. LL.B. (Hons.) [Cyber Security] and KIIT School of Law’s 10-semester B.Sc. LL.B. (Hons.) programme.

quick_facts.label: Entry qualification
quick_facts.value: Admission normally follows Class 12 (10+2) or an equivalent qualification. For the CLAT 2026 UG route, the Consortium specified 45% marks for General candidates and 40% for SC/ST/PwD candidates, with no upper age limit; individual institutions can impose programme-specific subject and category rules.

quick_facts.label: CLAT availability
quick_facts.value: CLAT is an admission route at participating National Law Universities, not a universal entrance requirement for every B.Sc. LL.B. (Hons.) provider. The CLAT 2026 allotment list included NLIU Bhopal’s B.Sc. LL.B. (Hons.) [Cyber Security] and WBNUJS Kolkata’s B.Sc. LL.B. (Hons.) [Criminology and Forensic].

quick_facts.label: Academic character
quick_facts.value: The science component and honours focus vary by university. Published examples include cyber security, biotechnology and forensics; NLIU describes coverage of Bar Council of India compulsory papers alongside cybersecurity honours and optional papers.

quick_facts.label: Professional recognition check
quick_facts.value: Before enrolling, applicants should verify that the particular five-year integrated law course and institution are recognised/approved for Bar Council of India purposes. BCI states that it recognises the 10+2 plus five-year integrated-law pattern and that its AIBE process requires study at a BCI-recognised university or BCI-approved affiliated college.

sections.heading: Eligibility: Class 12 first, then institution-specific science requirements
paragraphs: This is an integrated entry-after-school law degree, rather than a course entered after completing a separate B.Sc. For the CLAT 2026 UG cycle, candidates needed a passed 10+2/equivalent examination with at least 45% marks (40% for SC/ST/PwD), and candidates taking the qualifying examination in March or April 2026 could apply provisionally. The Consortium stated that there was no upper age limit for that cycle.

The degree’s science-oriented identity does not create one uniform all-India subject rule. For example, KIIT’s published B.Sc. LL.B. (Hons.) eligibility required 10+2/equivalent and minimum marks, while other institutions may prescribe Mathematics, Computer Science, science-stream, domicile, reservation, or additional-test conditions. Read the current prospectus of the exact university and programme; CLAT eligibility alone is not an offer of admission.
bullets: For a CLAT-participating option, meet both the current CLAT UG eligibility rules and the allotted university’s rules.
For a non-CLAT option, use that university’s own entrance/application rules rather than assuming CLAT is accepted.
Confirm that the exact course is a regular, BCI-recognised/approved integrated law programme if legal practice is a goal.

sections.heading: Duration and study mode
paragraphs: The standard format is five academic years, commonly organised as 10 semesters. KIIT explicitly publishes a five-year/10-semester structure, and the National Forensic Sciences University’s Goa campus also lists B.Sc. LL.B. (Hons.) as a five-year programme.

BCI has stated that three-year and five-year LL.B. programmes are regular courses and has warned that correspondence, open/distance-learning and online programmes presented as LL.B.-type legal qualifications are not BCI-approved. Applicants should therefore check the delivery mode and the BCI status of the exact course, not merely the degree title.
bullets: Plan for a full-time, five-year integrated programme.
Do not assume a distance or online law programme will support advocate-enrolment goals.

sections.heading: Curriculum: core law plus a science-and-technology pathway
paragraphs: The legal core typically combines foundational and professional subjects with skills training, while the science strand differentiates one institution’s B.Sc. LL.B. from another. NLIU states that its Cyber Security programme covers all compulsory papers prescribed by the BCI and adds cybersecurity-oriented honours and optional papers, including attention to data protection, blockchain, artificial intelligence and multimedia protection.

Published WBNUJS material illustrates how an interdisciplinary curriculum can work in practice: alongside legal methods, torts, contracts, constitutional law, criminal law, evidence, company law, intellectual-property law, drafting, ADR, professional ethics and trial advocacy, it lists forensic science, cyber law and cybercrime, cyber forensics, DNA technology, ballistics and criminal psychology. This is an example, not a nationwide prescribed syllabus.

Other universities frame the science component differently. KIIT lists Biotechnology, Cyber Security and Forensics as specialisations, so applicants should compare the semester-wise course structure, laboratory/technical exposure, clinics, research requirements, internships and available electives before choosing a school.
bullets: Expect the balance between science and law to vary substantially across institutions.
Inspect whether the advertised specialisation is a named degree branch, a set of electives, an honours track, or only a marketing focus.
For technology/IP ambitions, look for subjects such as data protection, cyber law, intellectual property, evidence, research methods and relevant technical coursework.

sections.heading: Admission: CLAT route for participating NLUs; direct routes elsewhere
paragraphs: CLAT can lead to selected B.Sc. LL.B. (Hons.) seats at participating NLUs. The Consortium’s CLAT 2026 first provisional allotment page specifically listed NLIU Bhopal’s B.Sc. LL.B. (Hons.) [Cyber Security] and WBNUJS Kolkata’s B.Sc. LL.B. (Hons.) [Criminology and Forensic]. The same Consortium instructs candidates to check each NLU’s brochure for its intake, reservation policy, fees, courses and detailed rules.

For the CLAT 2026 cycle, the test was an offline two-hour, 120-question multiple-choice paper covering English, Current Affairs/GK, Legal Reasoning, Logical Reasoning and Quantitative Techniques, with 0.25 negative marking per incorrect answer. That format and all dates are cycle-specific and should not be treated as a forecast for later CLAT admissions.

Many private/deemed universities admit through their own application or entrance process. Therefore, a candidate should build a separate application checklist for each target institution instead of treating CLAT as mandatory for the degree title.
bullets: Shortlist the exact specialisation first, then identify whether it uses CLAT or a direct institutional route.
Check the current prospectus for seat matrix, reservation, subject prerequisites, fees, document deadlines and counselling rules.
Treat published fee and deadline information as year-specific; verify it directly with the admitting institution before paying.

sections.heading: After graduation: postgraduate study, practice, specialist registration and legal roles
paragraphs: For an LL.M., an integrated LL.B. graduate can use university-specific admissions routes, including CLAT PG at participating institutions. In the CLAT 2026 PG rules, the benchmark was an LL.B. degree/equivalent with 50% marks, or 45% for SC/ST/PwD candidates; final-year candidates could apply provisionally. Individual universities may set further requirements or offer specialised LL.M. programmes.

For litigation advocacy, the degree itself is not the whole professional pathway. The Advocates Act provides for admission to a State roll for eligible holders of a law degree from a university recognised for the Act’s purposes, subject to statutory conditions. BCI’s AIBE guidance also stresses that candidates must be studying at, or have graduated from, a BCI-recognised university or BCI-approved affiliated college. Follow the current State Bar Council enrolment and AIBE notification applicable when graduating.

The Patent Agent Examination is a potentially relevant specialist route only where the graduate satisfies the statutory science/engineering/technology qualification requirement in addition to the other conditions. The Patent Office manual states that registration requires Indian citizenship, age above 21, a bachelor’s degree in science or engineering from a recognised Indian university (or other Central-Government-specified equivalent qualification), and passing the Controller General’s examination. Confirm that the credential actually awarded by the B.Sc. LL.B. programme meets that requirement before relying on this pathway.

Judicial-services examinations, JAG recruitment, PSU legal-officer posts, corporate counsel and in-house legal roles are separate recruitment pathways: their vacancies set their own age, citizenship, marks, registration, experience, medical and selection requirements. A science-law specialisation may be especially relevant to technology, data-protection, cyber, forensic, biotechnology and IP work, but it does not guarantee a job or waive employer/exam eligibility rules.
bullets: LL.M.: compare specialisations and each university’s current eligibility and entrance route.
Litigation advocate: verify BCI recognition, then complete the State Bar Council/AIBE steps in force at graduation.
Patent agent: verify the actual science-degree eligibility before applying; the integrated law title alone is not a substitute for the statutory condition.
Judiciary, JAG, PSU and corporate roles: use the recruiting authority’s current official notification, not generic career claims.
